header.title: Iwona Loureiro Studio - Interior Design & Architecture

about.title: ABOUT ME
about.story1: I founded my own design studio after 13 years of working as an architect in Kraków. Although interior design wasn't part of my original career plans, I eventually realized that it's the field that brings me the greatest satisfaction and sense of purpose. With every new project, I became more confident that this was the right path — one that allows me to combine my professional experience, aesthetic sensitivity, and the desire to create something truly meaningful for others. I believe that the spaces we live in have a real impact on our well-being — they're where we work, relax, sleep, cook, and spend time with loved ones. That's why I create interiors that are not only functional and comfortable, but also visually pleasing and enjoyable to be in. Spaces that simply feel right.
about.story2: I am a graduate of the Faculty of Architecture and Urban Planning at the Cracow University of Technology and hold a license for unlimited architectural design. Art has always been a part of my life — painting, drawing, and graphic design have been lifelong passions that continue to inspire my work. Years of professional experience, aesthetic intuition, and an openness to new ideas allow me to design interiors tailored to the individual needs and lifestyles of my clients — with professionalism and a touch of uniqueness. Every project is a new story, a new challenge, and an opportunity to create something truly special.

descriptions.krakow-lwowska:
Type: Apartment in a tenement building

Scope: Full conceptual and executive design

Zone: Apartment

Concept: A contemporary apartment with a deep green accent for someone who loves mountain hiking and cycling. An apartment design in which the simplicity of form is complemented by expressive details and a refined palette. The interior combines contemporary minimalism with subtle references to mid-century aesthetics, creating a coherent, timeless living space. The main goal was to create an orderly, functional interior with clearly defined zones: day, private, and work. Another priority was to make the most of the space while keeping a sense of visual lightness—especially in the bathroom and living room, where a dedicated workspace also had to be carved out. The interior follows a contemporary modernist language with clear inspiration from the 1960s and 1970s. A neutral, light base is broken up by deep, saturated green, present in the kitchen cabinetry and the shower area. It is paired with warm wood tones, soft beiges, and subtle black accents. Together, they create an elegant yet relaxed atmosphere—modern, but also welcoming and “homey.” The design is built on contrasting yet harmonious materials: matte fronts in deep green, natural wood with a warm finish, light tiles with a simple pattern, terrazzo flooring in the bathroom, and black details in fixtures and accessories. The bedroom introduces a stronger accent with forest-themed wallpaper that adds depth and a more intimate mood. The result is a calm, orderly, and functional interior where every element has a clear purpose. The subtle base is punctuated by bold accents, so the space never feels monotonous while remaining timeless and easy to live with day to day.
descriptions.krakow-piasta:
Type: Developer-finished apartment

Scope: Full conceptual and executive design

Zone: Hall, WC, bathroom, kitchen with living room

Concept: The interior design combines a minimalist, contemporary aesthetic with the warmth of natural materials and a subtle decorative accent in terrazzo. The owners’ key goal was a space that is easy to live in every day, with generous storage, in timeless forms and colors. The main aim was therefore a functional, visually coherent, and enduring interior, while introducing individual character through details and shapes. It was essential to keep the space feeling light while adding distinctive elements that define its identity. The layout reads as one coherent, harmonious day zone with clearly marked areas. The scheme pairs a minimalist base with softer forms and restrained color accents, creating a welcoming, timeless environment. Custom joinery—designed individually and tailored to the space and the users’ needs—is central to the project. In the day area it forms an orderly structure that integrates storage and a media zone. The palette is built on light, neutral beiges and off-white, paired with natural wood. Terrazzo in the bathroom is the standout feature: it adds rhythm and color while staying consistent with the rest of the apartment. The project brings together contemporary minimalism with a warm, domestic feel. Simple volumes and a calm composition are enriched with thoughtful details. The interior is restrained but not monotonous—texture contrasts and subtle color touches give it character. The result is a calm, functional, visually light space that supports everyday comfort while keeping a clear, recognizable identity.
descriptions.krakow-siewna:
Type: Existing apartment

Scope: Full conceptual and executive design for the modernization

Zone: Kitchen

Concept: A contemporary kitchen in an older block—functionality that makes the difference. This project shows how an ordinary, often overlooked space in a typical building can become a comfortable, attractive place for everyday life. The aim was to make the most of the available floor area and bring in a fresh, modern character—without unnecessary complexity or blowing the budget. The interior relies on simple, proven solutions. The kitchen cabinetry was planned to maximize storage while keeping a light visual feel. Warm wood paired with matte blue fronts adds coziness and balance, and a light worktop brightens the whole composition. A small dining zone matters too: a round table with comfortable chairs, and a bench along the wall that conceals extra storage drawers. It works equally well for quick breakfasts and daily gatherings. Bold accents shape the mood—a geometric floor pattern plus red lighting, table, and chairs that add energy without taking over the space. Lighting plays a major role—both practical under-cabinet light and decorative layers that build atmosphere, together with a large mirror that adds depth. This is a project for people who want a good-looking, practical interior without extra cost or fuss. It proves that even in a typical block apartment you can create a space tailored to your needs—attractive, comfortable, and simply well designed.
descriptions.odporyszow:
Type: Single-family house

Scope: Full conceptual and executive design

Zone: Kitchen with dining area, living room with access to the mezzanine

Concept: The design responds to the need for a bright, calm living space where the architecture of the house and the interior complement each other. A key premise was to unlock the potential of the open, double-height volume and extensive glazing on each wall, allowing natural light to fill the interior freely and emphasize its spaciousness and airiness. At the heart of the home, the living room, dining area, and kitchen read as one coherent whole. The layout supports everyday routines as well as time spent with family and friends. The minimalist kitchen cabinetry was designed to stay almost invisible—concealed storage and uniform fronts bring order to the space and lend it an elegant character. Tall cabinetry also integrates a discreet door to the pantry. Natural materials such as light wood, stone, and soft textiles create a warm, inviting atmosphere. The central kitchen island works both as a practical workspace and as a social hub for daily interaction. In the living room, the fireplace and a soft modular sofa define a comfortable zone for relaxation. Subtle lighting completes the scheme: several light sources distributed across the room make it easy to shape the mood of the interior to suit the residents. The result brings together a contemporary aesthetic with everyday functionality—a space that feels refined yet genuinely livable.
descriptions.jankowka:
Type: Single-family house

Scope: Conceptual design for attic conversion + visualizations

Zone: Usable attic level

Concept: A residential attic design where functionality meets a soft, nostalgically warm 1960s–70s aesthetic. The clients needed a calm space for work, a bedroom, and a compact bathroom. The main goal was a functional interior that supports comfortable work while preserving the cozy character of an attic. The key challenge was organizing space under the slopes, making the most of hard-to-reach areas and existing roof windows. In the central zone, a study for one owner was designed with a generous desk and a small relaxation nook. The larger office is for the second owner: it combines ample workspace with room for her hobby—handicrafts in various forms. The space includes plenty of storage for materials and tools, supporting order and a comfortable workflow. Custom joinery tailored to the slopes and a partly glazed upper wall separating the two work areas are essential to the scheme, combining display, storage, and work functions. A light palette and natural materials visually enlarge the interior and emphasize airiness despite typical attic angles. A guest room and bathroom are integral to the project—simple in form but full of character. Warm, saturated wall colors, wallpaper, and wooden furniture add intimacy. A subtle play of color and geometric tiles give the bathroom presence without overpowering the whole. Natural wood with a pronounced grain is the dominant material throughout, balanced with light surfaces and soft light so the richer palette never feels heavy. The result is a space with a clear identity—warm, calm, and suited to daily life. Despite demanding geometry, the attic has become a fully usable, comfortable place to live, work, and rest.

services.title: SERVICES
services.description: Interior design is both my passion and my area of expertise. I create spaces that combine aesthetics with functionality, always tailored to the individual needs of my clients. Whether I'm working on an apartment, a house, or a commercial interior, my goal is to design a place that simply feels good to be in. Every project is carried out with the utmost care and attention to detail. I listen closely to my clients expectations, offer inspiration, and work collaboratively to find the best solutions. With years of experience, knowledge of current trends, and a practical approach, I provide comprehensive, high-quality design services.
services.consultationDesc: During our first meeting, we'll discuss your expectations and vision. I'll learn more about your needs, inspirations, and lifestyle, and present my offer in more detail.
services.conceptDesc: I'll prepare functional layouts in 2D format, divided into zones. You'll also receive interior visualizations that showcase proposed colors, materials, furniture, and lighting.
services.detailedDesc: I'll develop a complete execution-ready interior design package, including: functional plans with descriptions, lighting layouts, sections and elevations, custom furniture drawings with dimensions, equipment specifications, and final visualizations.
services.supervisionDesc: As part of my supervision service, I will ensure the project is implemented according to the documentation, assist in selecting specific materials, colors, and solutions, and provide on-site support during construction when needed.
services.ctaDesc: Let's discuss how we can bring your vision to life.

contact.title: CONTACT
contact.intro: Let's discuss your next project and how we can help bring your vision to life.
